content.text:
ttyview is a mobile-first plugin platform for viewing tmux sessions in a browser. The CC view you're looking at right now is one of the bundled plugins — it reads the JSONL transcript from disk and renders it as chat bubbles.

The whole UI is plugins. You can install themes, header widgets, alternative terminal renderers, or your own keyboard accessory rows from the Discover tab in Settings (⚙). Try Cmd-K (or Ctrl-K) to open the command palette.

message.content: Does it actually drive Claude Code from a phone?

content.thinking: The user wants concrete proof this works on mobile. I should mention the specific design decisions that make it phone-friendly.

content.text:
Yes — that's the primary design target. A few of the mobile-specific pieces:

• The Quick Keys plugin adds an Esc / Tab / Ctrl-C / arrow row above the input, so you can drive CC's TUI without those keys on the soft keyboard.
• The cell-grid renderer uses CSS containment + content-visibility for paint isolation.
• The Settings overlay pins to the top of the visible viewport (uses dvh) so the close button is always reachable when the URL bar is showing.
• You can install plugins straight from the phone via the Discover tab.

Open Settings → Plugins → Themes and try Solarized Dark or Nord on this demo to see live theme switching.

message.content: Cool. Show me a tool call.

content.text: Sure — here's a simulated Bash tool call you'd see in a real session:

In a real CC session those would render as a nested tool block here, with the user message containing the tool result inline. The plugin handles all of that.

You're done with the demo — try the command palette next (Cmd-K), or install another plugin from Discover.
